title: SectionAnalysis Object
description: Represents any existing Section Analysis that exist in the design.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: A method that deletes this Analysis.

methods.name: flip
methods.description: A property that flips which side of the part is cut away by the section. This is a convenience method that results in flipping the Z axis of the transform while maintaining a valid rectangular coordinate system. You can directly manipulate the transform matrix to have the same effect.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: A property that returns the collection of attributes associated with this Analysis.

properties.name: cutPlane
properties.description: A property that gets and sets the planar entity used to define the cut plane and can be either a planar BRepFace or a ConstructionPlane object.

properties.name: entityToken
properties.description: Returns a token for the Analysis object. The token can be saved and used later with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same Analysis.   When using entity tokens, it's crucial to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. For example, you can have two different token strings obtained from the same entity at different times, and when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that, you should never compare entity tokens as a way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

properties.name: initialPosition
properties.description: Returns the matrix that describes the initial position and orientation of the specified cut plane entity. Any additional offsets or rotations are defined by a transformation matrix that is applied to this initial position. That matrix can be obtained and set using the transform property.

properties.name: isHatchShown
properties.description: A property that gets and sets if a hatch pattern should be shown on the section.

properties.name: isLightBulbOn
properties.description: A property that gets and sets if the display is enabled for this Analysis object. If false, this analysis will be hidden. If true and the IsLightBulbOn property of the Analyses object is True the Analysis will be visible.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: isVisible
properties.description: Gets if this Analysis is currently visible in the graphics window. The visibility is controlled by a combination of the isLightBulbOn properties of the Analyses collection object and the Analysis object. If both are true, the Analysis will be visible.

properties.name: name
properties.description: A property that gets and sets the name of the analysis. If you use a name that is not unique, Fusion will automatically append a number to the name to make it unique.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: sectionColor
properties.description: A property that gets and sets the color of the section. A value of null indicates the component color should be used. The opacity value of the color is ignored.

properties.name: transform
properties.description: The initial position of the section plane is defined by the specified cut plane entity. Any offsets or rotations are defined by a transformation matrix that is applied to the initial position. This property allows you to get and set the transformation matrix.

attributes.title: SectionAnalysis.attributes Property
attributes.description: A property that returns the collection of attributes associated with this Analysis.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sectionAnalysis_var.attributes

cutPlane.title: SectionAnalysis.cutPlane Property
cutPlane.description: A property that gets and sets the planar entity used to define the cut plane and can be either a planar BRepFace or a ConstructionPlane object.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sectionAnalysis_var.cutPlane

# Set the value of the property.
sectionAnalysis_var.cutPlane = propertyValue

entityToken.description: Returns a token for the Analysis object. The token can be saved and used later with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same Analysis. When using entity tokens, it's crucial to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. For example, you can have two different token strings obtained from the same entity at different times, and when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that, you should never compare entity tokens as a way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = sectionAnalysis_var.entityToken

transform.title: SectionAnalysis.transform Property
transform.description: The initial position of the section plane is defined by the specified cut plane entity. Any offsets or rotations are defined by a transformation matrix that is applied to the initial position. This property allows you to get and set the transformation matrix.

classType.title: SectionAnalysis.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

deleteMe.title: SectionAnalysis.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: A method that deletes this Analysis.
deleteMe.return_description: Returns true if the delete was successful.

flip.title: SectionAnalysis.flip Method
flip.description: A property that flips which side of the part is cut away by the section. This is a convenience method that results in flipping the Z axis of the transform while maintaining a valid rectangular coordinate system. You can directly manipulate the transform matrix to have the same effect.
